item.title: Wear Intention, Attract Alignment: The Ultimate Guide to the Selenite Clarity Bracelet
item.author: Samriddhi Reiki Foundation (SRF)
item.content:encoded:
In the relentless hustle of modern life, our energetic bodies are constantly under siege. From the moment we wake up and check our phones to the moment we finally close our eyes after a long, stressful day, we absorb an astonishing amount of energetic debris. We pick up on the stress of our coworkers, the anxiety radiating from the daily news, and the heavy, stagnant energy that lingers in crowded spaces. Over time, this energetic pollution clouds our minds, drains our physical vitality, and severs our connection to our higher spiritual selves.We try everything to find our center again — meditation apps, weekend retreats, and endless cups of chamomile tea. While these are excellent practices, what if you could wear a constant, powerful energetic shield on your wrist? What if you could carry an aura of profound peace with you into every single boardroom, grocery store, and difficult conversation?This is where the magic of crystal healing truly shines. And when it comes to supreme energetic purification, there is one stone that stands absolutely unrivaled: Selenite.At Riddhi Siddhi Crystals, we believe that jewelry should be more than just a beautiful accessory; it should be a powerful spiritual tool. That is the exact philosophy behind our stunning Selenite Clarity Bracelet. In this comprehensive guide, we will explore the profound benefits of wearing a selenite bracelet, how it acts as the ultimate energetic cleanser, and how you can combine this powerful stone with the holistic therapies offered at the Samriddhi Reiki Foundation to completely transform your life.Before we can understand the profound benefits of wearing a selenite bracelet, we must first understand what this extraordinary mineral actually is.Selenite is a crystalline variety of the mineral gypsum. It is famous for its breathtaking, ethereal appearance. High-quality selenite is beautifully translucent, possessing a pearlescent, silky sheen that seems to glow from within. Because of this captivating luminescence, it is often referred to by healers as “liquid light.”The name Selenite is derived from Selene, the ancient Greek goddess of the moon. This is incredibly fitting, as the stone has a distinctively lunar, feminine energy. Just as the moon’s gravity pulls the ocean tides, the energetic gravity of selenite gently pulls stagnant, toxic energy out of the human aura, replacing it with the calm, illuminating glow of moonlight.Historically, selenite has been revered by mystics, shamans, and healers for thousands of years. It is considered a high-vibration crystal, meaning it operates at an incredibly fast energetic frequency. When you introduce a high-vibration crystal into your personal energy field, it forces your lower, denser vibrations (such as fear, anger, and anxiety) to rise up and match its frequency. This makes selenite one of the most powerful tools on earth for spiritual ascension and energetic clearing.
The Core Spiritual and Emotional Benefits of a Selenite Bracelet When you choose to wear the Selenite Clarity Bracelet from Riddhi Siddhi Crystals, you are not just making a fashion statement. You are making a conscious choice to actively protect and elevate your energy field every single day. Here are the most profound benefits of wearing a selenite bracelet:**## 1. The Ultimate Cleanser of Negative Energy **Imagine taking a long, hot shower after a grueling, sweaty workout. You watch all the dirt and grime wash down the drain, leaving you feeling fresh and renewed. A selenite bracelet does exactly this, but for your energetic body.Selenite is universally recognized in the crystal healing community as the ultimate cleanser of negative energy. Throughout your day, you inevitably interact with “energy vampires” — people or situations that drain your joy and leave you feeling exhausted. Wearing an authentic selenite crystal bracelet creates a continuous flow of purifying energy around your aura. It acts as an energetic filter, preventing the heavy, toxic energy of others from attaching to your field.Furthermore, selenite is famous for being a self-cleansing stone. Unlike most crystals that absorb negative energy and require frequent smudging or salt baths to clear, selenite’s vibration is so high that negative energy cannot attach to it. It constantly purifies itself, and by extension, it purifies you.*## 2. Enhancing Mental Clarity and Focus * Have you ever experienced “brain fog” — that frustrating state where you simply cannot concentrate, make decisions, or organize your thoughts? Brain fog is rarely a physical issue; it is usually a symptom of a cluttered, overwhelmed mental energy field.As its name implies, the Selenite Clarity Bracelet is a master of cutting through the mental noise. When worn on the wrist, the high-frequency energy of the selenite travels directly through your meridian lines, clearing the energetic cobwebs from your mind. It promotes a state of sharp, pristine mental clarity.If you are a student preparing for exams, a professional navigating complex projects, or simply someone trying to make a difficult life decision, the selenite bracelet for mental clarity and focus is an indispensable tool. It quiets the chaotic chatter of the ego, allowing you to access the deep, quiet wisdom of your intuition.*## 3. Creating a Calming, Peaceful Aura (Relief from Anxiety) * We live in an age of chronic anxiety. Our nervous systems are constantly trapped in a state of “fight or flight.” Selenite possesses an incredibly soothing, cooling, and calming energy.When you slip the Selenite Clarity Bracelet onto your wrist, it is akin to taking a deep, slow breath. The stone emits a frequency of profound peace. It is one of the best crystals for anxiety because it gently coaxes the nervous system out of its hyper-aroused state. If you find yourself in a stressful situation — perhaps a tense meeting or an overwhelming social event — simply placing your other hand over the selenite beads and taking three deep breaths can instantly ground your energy and stop a panic attack in its tracks.
**4. Unblocking and Activating the Crown Chakra ** In the ancient chakra system, the Crown Chakra (Sahasrara) is located at the very top of the head. It is our energetic gateway to the divine, to universal consciousness, and to our highest spiritual selves. When the Crown Chakra is blocked, we feel cynical, disconnected from our life’s purpose, and profoundly alone in the universe.Selenite is the ultimate Crown Chakra stone. The “liquid light” frequency of the selenite bracelet shoots a pillar of pure, white energy straight up through your central channel, blasting through any blockages in the upper chakras. Wearing this bracelet consistently keeps your Crown Chakra wide open, ensuring that you are constantly receiving divine inspiration, spiritual guidance, and a profound sense of universal connection.
**How to Care for Your Selenite Clarity Bracelet When you purchase a natural selenite bracelet from crystals.samriddhireikifoundation.cloud, you are investing in a sacred spiritual tool. To ensure that your bracelet lasts a lifetime and continues to vibrate at its highest potential, you must understand how to care for it properly.
The Golden Rule: Do Not Get Selenite Wet! ** This is the most critical piece of information you must know about selenite. On the Mohs scale of mineral hardness (which ranges from 1 to 10), selenite sits at a very soft 2. More importantly, selenite is a water-soluble gypsum crystal.If you submerge your selenite bracelet in water, it will begin to dissolve, lose its beautiful pearlescent luster, and eventually break apart.You must always remove your Selenite Clarity Bracelet before:Swimming in the ocean or a pool.Engaging in heavy workouts where you will sweat profusely.Applying lotions or perfumes to your wrists.
How to Cleanse and Charge Your Selenite Bracelet ** As mentioned earlier, selenite is a self-cleansing crystal, meaning it does not hold onto negative energy like a sponge. However, even selenite benefits from an occasional energetic “recharge” to keep its vibration operating at peak performance.Because you cannot use water or salt water to cleanse it, the best way to charge a selenite bracelet is through the power of moonlight. During the night of the Full Moon, place your bracelet on a windowsill where it will be bathed in lunar light overnight. The feminine, intuitive energy of the moon perfectly complements the selenite, recharging it to its brightest, most powerful state.You can also physically clean the bracelet if it gets dusty by gently wiping the beads with a very soft, completely dry microfiber cloth.
Synergy: Combining Selenite with Reiki Healing While the Selenite Clarity Bracelet is incredibly powerful on its own, its true miraculous potential is unlocked when you combine it with the holistic healing therapies offered at our parent organization, the Samriddhi Reiki Foundation.Reiki and selenite are a match made in energetic heaven. Because Reiki channels universal life force energy to clear blockages, and selenite acts as an energetic purifier, using them together creates a compounding, synergistic effect.Here is how you can use your new Riddhi Siddhi Crystals bracelet to amplify your Reiki journey:*1. Wear It During Distance Healing Sessions * If you have booked a remote energy healing session with one of the Grand Masters at the Samriddhi Reiki Foundation, wear your selenite bracelet during the appointment. The high vibration of the selenite will keep your aura incredibly clear and receptive, allowing the Reiki energy to penetrate much deeper into your physical and emotional bodies. The selenite acts as a perfect runway, welcoming the distant energy in.*2. Post-Healing Protection * After a profound Reiki session, your chakras are wide open, and your aura is beautifully clear. However, stepping back out into the chaotic world can quickly undo this delicate work if you are not careful. By wearing your Selenite Clarity Bracelet immediately after a session, you cast an energetic shield over your newly cleansed aura. The bracelet acts as a spiritual bodyguard, ensuring that you maintain the high-vibrational state achieved during your Reiki therapy.*3. Deepening Your Own Meditation and Reiki Practice * If you are a student taking one of the online Reiki courses (Level 1, 2, or Grand Mastership) through the foundation, your selenite bracelet is an essential study companion. Wearing the bracelet while practicing self-Reiki or meditating helps clear the mental chatter, instantly connecting you to the frequency of the Crown Chakra. It opens your intuitive channels, making it much easier to feel and direct the flow of Reiki energy through your own hands.*Why Choose Riddhi Siddhi Crystals? * The internet is flooded with cheap, mass-produced crystal jewelry. However, when it comes to energy healing, authenticity and intention are absolutely everything. A fake, glass, or chemically treated stone holds zero energetic frequency and will do nothing to protect your aura.When you buy a selenite bracelet online from Riddhi Siddhi Crystals, you are guaranteeing the highest standard of spiritual quality.*1. 100% Authentic, Natural Gemstones * We ethically source only the highest grade, 100% natural selenite for our Clarity Bracelets. Every single bead is carefully inspected for its purity, luster, and energetic resonance. You are wearing a true piece of the ancient earth.*2. Energized and Cleansed Before Shipping * What truly sets Riddhi Siddhi Crystals apart is our connection to the Samriddhi Reiki Foundation. Before your bracelet is placed in its packaging and shipped to your door, it is not just sitting in a warehouse. Every single bracelet is energetically cleansed and infused with powerful Reiki blessings by our certified Reiki Masters. When you open your package, you will immediately feel the high-vibrational, loving energy radiating from the stones. Your bracelet arrives absolutely ready to protect and align your energy field.*3. A Deep Commitment to Your Spiritual Journey * We do not just sell jewelry; we are dedicated to your holistic wellness. Riddhi Siddhi Crystals is part of a larger ecosystem of healing designed to empower you. From the stunning crystals on your wrist to the profound chakra alignments offered on our main website, we provide all the tools you need to achieve total spiritual mastery.
Conclusion: Wear Your Intention Your energy is your most valuable currency. Where your energy goes, your life flows. If you are constantly allowing negative people, stressful situations, and chaotic environments to drain your vitality, you will never have the power to manifest the beautiful, peaceful life you truly deserve.It is time to draw a line in the sand. It is time to declare that your peace is sacred.The Selenite Clarity Bracelet is your daily armor. It is a beautiful, elegant reminder to “Wear intention, and attract alignment.” By simply sliding these luminous beads onto your wrist every morning, you are commanding the universe to keep your aura clear, your mind sharp, and your heart peaceful.Do not spend another day feeling drained and disconnected. Elevate your frequency today.Visit our official crystal boutique at Crystals Samriddhi Reiki Foundation to order your authentic, Reiki-infused Selenite Clarity Bracelet right now.And if you are ready to take your healing journey to the absolute highest level, be sure to explore the life-changing holistic services and certified courses at our main home, the Samriddhi Reiki Foundation.Your purest, most vibrant life is waiting. Claim it today.

item.title: Trash Panda DB - A Pure Crystal embedded SQL database with Raft replication and crystal-db compatibility
item.author: weirdbricks
item.content:encoded: I wanted to see how far Claude could take me and I had it write an embedded SQL database with Raft replication and crystal-db compatibility - it’s entirely written in Crystal with no external dependencies. I also used other models through Claude Code such as GLM and Deepseek V4 Flash.I’m currently using this for my project, Dirless, without issues.

item.title: I Spent 6 Months Working With Chakra Stones — Here's What Actually Happened
item.author: Kui Luo
item.content:encoded:
About seven months ago, I was sleeping maybe four hours a night. Not because of insomnia exactly — more like my brain just refused to shut down. I'd tried melatonin, magnesium supplements, cutting screen time after 9pm. Nothing stuck. A friend who runs a wellness studio in Austin suggested I look into chakra stones. I was skeptical. I'm a data analyst by training — I don't believe in things I can't measure.But I was desperate enough to try anything.So I bought a set of seven crystals aligned with the seven chakras and committed to actually using them daily for six months. No skipping days. I kept a sleep journal, tracked my energy levels on a 1-10 scale every morning, and noted any changes in mood or focus. Here's what I found.
Month 1: Starting With the Root I started with root chakra stones — specifically black tourmaline and red jasper. The idea is that your root chakra governs your sense of safety and groundedness. Without it being balanced, everything above it is supposedly unstable too.The first two weeks, nothing happened. I put the stones under my pillow at night and carried black tourmaline in my pocket during the day. I felt like I was performing a ritual for no reason. My sleep score averaged 4.2/10 for the first 14 days, tracked via my Oura ring.Around day 18, something shifted. I noticed I was falling asleep about 20 minutes faster than before. Not earth-shattering, but consistent. By the end of month one, my average sleep score had crept up to 5.6/10.Was it the stones or just placebo? Honestly, I still don't know. But I kept going.
Months 2-3: The Sacral and Solar Plexus Experiment Here's the thing nobody tells you about crystal work: the routine itself becomes a form of mindfulness. Ten minutes in the morning holding a warm carnelian stone, focusing on my breathing, became a ritual I actually looked forward to. I wasn't just "playing with rocks" — I was meditating. The stone was just an anchor.During these two months, my energy levels (self-reported, 1-10 scale) averaged 6.8, up from 5.1 in the first month. I also noticed I was less reactive to stressful situations at work. Whether that's the crystals, the meditation, or just me getting better at handling stress, the outcome was real.
Month 4: The Heart Chakra Shift I've always had a hard time with vulnerability. Green aventurine and rose quartz were the recommended heart chakra crystals. I placed rose quartz on my chest during my evening meditation for the entire month.This one surprised me. By week three, I had a conversation with my partner about something I'd been avoiding for literally two years. I can't attribute that entirely to a pink stone, but the consistent practice of sitting with my chest, breathing, and focusing on openness seemed to create space for honesty I hadn't accessed before.
Months 5-6: Third Eye and Crown I added amethyst and clear quartz for the third eye chakra. This was the most subtle phase. I didn't notice dramatic shifts, but I did start having more vivid dreams and felt generally more intuitive about small decisions — like knowing which project to prioritize at work.My final averages for month 6:Sleep score: 7.3/10 (up from 4.2)Energy level: 7.6/10 (up from 5.1)Self-reported stress: 3.2/10 (down from 7.8)Look, I can't tell you crystals have magical properties. What I can tell you is this: the practice of working with them created a daily meditation habit that measurably improved my sleep, energy, and stress levels. The stones themselves may be incidental. Or they may not be. I'm not qualified to say.What I am qualified to say is that I spent about $85 total on my crystal set, and the return on that investment — six months of better sleep, lower stress, and a consistent mindfulness practice — is hard to argue with.If you're curious but skeptical like I was, the best beginner resource I found was this guide. It breaks down each chakra, which stones correspond to it, and how to actually start using them without falling into the "buy everything at once" trap.One piece of advice I wish someone had given me: start with just one stone for one chakra. Don't buy a whole set. Pick the area of your life that needs the most attention — sleep, confidence, emotional balance — and work with that single stone for at least a month before adding more.I started with black tourmaline for my root chakra. It was the right call. Six months later, I still sleep with it under my pillow most nights.

item.title: Crystal 1.20.3 is released!
item.author: Crys
item.content:encoded: We are announcing a new Crystal release 1.20.3 with fixes for two security vulnerabilities in the YAML parsers. We recommend upgrading as soon as possible if your application processes YAML files from unknown origins.

item.title: Crystal 1.20.3 is released!
item.author: Julien Portalier
item.content:encoded: We are announcing a new Crystal release 1.20.3 with fixes for two security vulnerabilities in the parsers. We recommend upgrading as soon as possible if your application processes YAML files from unknown origins.The parsers use recursion to parse sequences and mappings but didn’t limit how deep they could be nested. A deeply nested YAML document could lead to stack overflows (#17107).Add to (#17107)The parsers were vulnerable to the billion laughs attack when expanding aliases, growing the document from 100 nodes to over 1 billion nodes for example. The parser had no issues parsing the document, but any attempt to walk the stack could walk the same nodes over and over, quickly slowing down applications.Add alias vs anchor ratio check to (#17109)Memoize anchor name in (#17108)We have been able to do all of this thanks to the continued support of 84codes and every other sponsor. To maintain and increase the development pace, donations and sponsorships are essential. OpenCollective is available for that.Reach out to crystal@manas.tech if you’d like to become a direct sponsor or find other ways to support Crystal. We thank you in advance!Contribute

item.title: Crystal Contexts
item.author: mgomes
item.content:encoded: Hey friends, I put together a little experimental shard that brings Go-style contexts to Crystal. They’re a nice tool for making certain kinds of programs (web servers especially) more robust.Because contexts are cooperative, this can live entirely as a shard. We don’t need runtime changes. The catch is that standard library classes like HTTP don’t respect them natively.My one reservation is the Go ergonomics: you end up threading a context through every method call, and I’m not sure there’s a way around it.Would love to hear what you all think and whether the structured concurrency work already has plans for a Crystal-flavored context library.

item.title: Nuklear ui Bindings for SDL3/OpenGL
item.author: guidipolito
item.content:encoded: I started working on these bindings for a personal project, and maybe they could be of help to someone.I still need to make a better wrapper, but it’s starting to work.I tried to use crystal_lib to generate the bindings, and even though it generated 90% of the bindings, in this case it didn’t get some structs and typedefs that I fixed/added. Is there an alternative generator?

item.title: Agent.cr - an agentic framework for OpenAI protocol
item.author: anykeyh
item.content:encoded: I had a project that needed a proper AI-assistant layer, and after digging through the shards ecosystem I couldn’t find anything that fit, so I built my own: .It’s heavily inspired by the excellent RubyLLM gem (kudos to them! A lot of the ergonomics are stolen with love ). It wraps any OpenAI-compatible streaming endpoint behind a fiber-based async interface, so never blocks your code. with automatic resolution (no manual while-loop), plus enable/disable mid-conversation — images, audio, text files, auto-detected by extension with typed chunks (content, reasoning tokens, tool-call deltas) with automatic history tracking + trimming (history, tool state, and prompt-cache affinity preserved)A middleware-style handler chain that wraps each pipeline stage: turn, streamed chunk, tool call, error… so you can inspect, mutate, or short-circuit. Handy for gating tool calls behind user confirmation, enforcing a token budget, or rewriting content in-flight.Pluggable provider abstraction (anything OpenAI-compatible: OpenAI, OpenRouter, local llama.cpp, Ollama…). The architecture is open for non-OpenAI protocols (Anthropic) but I’m not planning to implement them in the near future.What it do: . No plan in the near term; I don’t run MCP servers for this project. Maybe later for customer integrations, but not a priority.: embedding and STT endpoints as “extra” features, then I’ll tag .There are a couple of self-contained examples in . The fun one is a stupid little text-based RPG where the LLM is your Dungeon Master and Crystal tools enforce the rules. No cheating, not even for the DM:crystal run examples/rpg.cr # Note: Setup in .env.local first your ENV variable to your LLM There’s also if you want to see the middleware chain in action (token budgeting, tool confirmation, in-flight word replacement).Tested both locally (Strix Halo box running Qwen3.6) and over OpenRouter (Deepseek v4 & GLM5.2).The code was written with AI-assistance, but not vibe-coded (e.g. control on each steps). For those interested, globally AI is working well with Crystal Lang, except some confusions with Ruby methods.Would love to hear what you think !

item.title: A basic wrapper around shard
item.author: zendrx
item.content:encoded:
Crystal Package Manager — a tiny wrapper around that saves you from hunting URLs.I got tired of this workflow:Want to add to my projectSearch “kemal crystal shard”That’s 7 steps for something that should be 1.No browser. No copy-paste. No remembering URLs. is great at installing dependencies. But it doesn’t help you them. fills that gap and gets out of the way.curl -fsSl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slick-lab/cpm/main/install.sh | sudo bash # Initialize a project cpm init cpm init --cache # also creates ~/.cpm/cache.txt
# Search for packages cpm search kemal cpm search kemal --auto # pick top result automatically
# Add a dependency cpm add kemal
# Remove a dependency cpm remove kemal
# Then use shards as normal shards install shards prune shards update doesn’t replace . It just makes the part easier: queries GitHub for Crystal shards checks your cache, writes to handles the actual downloading and version resolutionWe keep a simple :kemal https://github.com/kemalcr/kemal pg https://github.com/will/crystal-pg No JSON. No metadata. Just names and URLs. is perfect for what it does: resolving versions and installing dependencies.But assumes you already know: helps with discovery. Then takes over.

item.title: Clear-cache effect?
item.author: hutou
item.content:encoded: Can the ‘crystal clear_cache’ command have a (very) significant impact on the size of the executable (and the compile time)?

item.title: Neonmem 0.9.7 is out.
item.author: Neonmem Dev Team
item.content:encoded:
1. A two-level importer — two kinds of "stuff," treated differently The big change. Your project doesn't come in one shape, so the importer no longer flattens it into one pile:Folders & files → a searchable knowledge pool. Your docs, code and notes are vectorised into a lossless, deduplicated facts pool — the same fact stated three ways becomes one fact, every source kept. Nothing is summarised away.Agent chats → typed memories. Point Neonmem at a Claude (or other agent) transcript and it pulls out only what's worth keeping — the decisions, dead-ends and rules — as clean, typed memories. A decision is stored as a decision; a dead-end stays a warning. The process-narration ("I read the file…", "please check…") is dropped. If a chat references a file on disk, that file is pulled into the pool automatically, with a memory that points back to it.The result is labelled honestly in the UI: (the pool) and (the kept decisions).
2. Grounded, offline recall 0.9.7 replaces the old embedder with , run as a fused graph through :Database-class retrieval quality on — no GPU, no PyTorch, no API key, no cloud.Every prompt walks memory in order — reflexes → short-term → long-term → facts pool — and answers from what you actually imported, or honestly says it doesn't know.This is the headline behaviour: ask and you get definition from docs — not the textbook expansion the model would otherwise guess. A memory that's occasionally wrong is worse than no memory at all, so the rule is: answer from the user's sources, or abstain. Never invent.Tag an import with a topic (e.g. ) and Neonmem mints one clean, canonical memory for it, linked back to the source — even when your docs never write the term verbatim, as long as they clearly describe it. If the corpus genuinely has nothing on a tag, it's left out rather than faked.Memories follow one : a single concise statement (ARC — your provisioning platform) linked to the full source, not a messy pile of raw chunks. Chat capture deduplicates through the same facts layer, so re-importing a conversation never doubles up.The importer keeps the inside the cartridge (content-addressed + compressed) — one file replaces the scattered docs and transcripts, and the facts are always rebuildable from ground truth.Opt-in AES-256-GCM encryption at rest — your whole corpus as a private vault.Imported knowledge is long-term and the project.
Built on (all open, permissively licensed) Embeddings: (Apache-2.0) via (MIT). Vector search: (MIT). Agent integration: the . Full attributions ship with every download. No third-party LLM, nothing phones home.Windows (signed installer + portable) and Linux (AppImage); macOS on the way. Local, private, and free for personal use.Import a project, then ask it the one thing your assistant always gets confidently wrong about your codebase. That question is the whole test.

item.title: Crystal-swim: a pure Crystal implementation of SWIM protocol
item.author: paulocoghi
item.content:encoded: For anyone building distributed systems that may scale to large clusters, I just published the shard which implements the SWIM protocol.(Scalable Weakly-consistent Infection-style Process Group Membership) A decentralized cluster membership and failure detection protocol.It solves a similar problem to consensus algorithms (like Raft or Paxos) by keeping a distributed system synchronized. However, while consensus requires over data, SWIM provides eventually consistent awareness of who is alive and who is dead.What is the main benefit? When eventual consistency is acceptable, using it allows the network load to stay flat regardless of cluster size.A 10-node cluster and a 1,000-node cluster cost each node about the same few UDP packets per second.Pure Crystal implementation It is made in pure Crystal, has no dependencies and includes all the Lifeguard extensions developed by HashiCorp, bringing the most refined version of it to the Crystal community.It will be useful on other open source projects I’m creating in Crystal as well, and which I will share here soon.Are there more members here working on distributed systems where this shard would be useful?

item.title: How I Built 200+ High-Performance Web Apps for US Businesses (And What I Learned)

item.title: Idiomatic Crystal OpenAPI client generator — redesign (powering a real Qdrant + RAG stack)
item.author: n-rodriguez
item.content:encoded:
Crystal’s client generator in OpenAPI Generator has been a beta for a while, and its output never really felt like Crystal — a flat class per tag, prefixed methods, twins, a global singleton client, and several constructs that didn’t even compile (or crashed at runtime) on real-world specs.What the output looks like now# Before — global singleton, prefixed methods, (value, status, headers) tuples api = Petstore::PetApi.new pets, status, headers = api.find_pets_by_status_with_http_info(["available"])
# After — per-instance client, namespaced sub-clients routed from the path client = Petstore::Client.new(host: "petstore.swagger.io") pets = client.pet.find_by_status(status: ["available"]).value And on a real spec, sub-clients nest naturally from the path:client = Qdrant::Api::Client.new(host: "localhost", scheme: "http") result = client.collections.points.search(/* ... */).value routed from the path (client.collections.points.search) instead of DcimApi#dcim_cable_terminations_list. — Connection#request(T) forall T; each operation is a short declarative call returning a typed . No duplicates (~38 lines of boilerplate per op → a handful). — every owns its own /. No global state. — one declarative macro replaces the per-model hierarchy; / via stdlib ; a shared mixin. that the unit fixtures never caught (Crystal only type-checks code it actually reaches): / → proper union wrappers that try each member (discriminator fast-path when present). The old wrapper d with no fields and on deserialise. → transparent (the old class with couldn’t be built by ). → children stop re-declaring inherited ivars (Crystal forbids re-annotating), forward via , and emit for polymorphism. Model s are topologically ordered., , responses, and — previously broken or missing. preserved across round-trips via JSON::Serializable::Unmapped.It’s not a synthetic redesign — it ships in productionThe generator powers a three-layer Qdrant stack written in Crystal: — the low-level Qdrant REST client, generated by this very generator from Qdrant’s full OpenAPI spec (~320 models, unions, named enums). The new samples/client/others/crystal-qdrant sample in the PR exercises the same path. — an idiomatic, RAG-oriented wrapper over it (anti-corruption layer — no generated type leaks into its public surface), tested in CI against a real Qdrant container. — a MCP server (hybrid semantic + keyword doc search for Claude Code / Cursor / …) that runs entirely on your own machine — your docs never leave it. Uses as its vector store.Most of the //named-enum/ bugs above were surfaced by generating against that full spec, not by the fixtures.petstore sample: → crystal-qdrant sample: → codegen unit tests: ; both samples cleanIf you generate Crystal clients from OpenAPI specs, I’d love for you to try the branch against your own spec and tell me where the generated API feels awkward — that’s the feedback that’ll make this good. PR is .

item.title: Termisu v0.5.0
item.author: omarluq
item.content:encoded: It’s been a while since I posted about Termisu. Looking back, the last forum announcement was for .For anyone who missed the earlier post: Termisu is a small Crystal library for building terminal UIs with a focus on simplicity, portability, and developer experience. It remains intentionally small and Crystal first, with zero runtime dependencies beyond the standard library and broad support across *nix systems.Today, we’re at , and the project has grown considerably.Since , Termisu has gained:A significantly more mature rendering and event systemBetter keyboard and mouse handlingResize, timer, tick, and mode change eventsEnhanced keyboard protocol supportMore robust terminal state managementHigh precision system timers for animation loopsInitial TypeScript and Bun bindings via Substantially expanded test coverageImproved CI, release automation, and developer toolingI’d also like to give a huge thank you to @wuestenfee (not sure if they are on the forum) and @hahwul for their contributions and help improving the project. Open source is always better when people show up and lend a hand We’re still pre 1.0 and there’s plenty left to do, but seeing how far the project has come since has been incredibly rewarding.As always, feedback, bug reports, API suggestions, and contributions are welcome.

item.title: myc(MyCompiler) — a small IR for building languages over LLVM, QBE, C
item.author: kostya
item.content:encoded: Simple DSL over LLVM/QBE.Your language AST → mycIR → [LLVM / QBE / C] → binary.Whole IR spec fits in 15 minutes of reading.Compiles to native code via LLVM, QBE, or C.Fast compilation, zero overhead.I was writing my own language and got tired of fighting with LLVM IR. SSA, phi nodes, basic blocks — X(.Usually when you write your own language, you first build a parser and generate an AST. Then comes the hell stage — translating your AST into LLVM or another backend. Myc takes on all that complexity.Stack-based opcodes are easy to emit from AST with a simple one-pass tree walk.You’re not locked into one backend. LLVM for speed, QBE for fast compiles, C for anywhere.Mandelbrot renderer from mandel.bf (by Erik Bosman). All IR represent the same program. Shows whether Myc adds overhead over direct backend usage. Running on Macbook M1 in benchmark/brainfuck-compiler.Quick Start (compile and run first program).echo 'FUNC main BODY PUSH "Hello myc\n" PRINTF 0 ENDFUNC' | crystal src/cli/llvm.cr r Alpha. But already powerful. All 3 backends work smoothly. 2900 tests pass.

item.title: Log: When to use emit vs. context
item.author: phil294
item.content:encoded: when do you use one over the other? I can’t really find docs about this. The following just prints both data structures, making me wonder why there are two different ways of doing it. The only difference I can see is that you can set context on a per-Fiber basis while emit sends volatile data.require "log" Log.with_context(x: { y: 1 }) do Log.error do |emitter| emitter.emit("...", a: { b: 2 }) end end While Crystal’s docs are usually fantastic, I have found the logging module hard to understand. Personally I would have loved more elaborate infos on the various building blocks. They are scattered among the different submodules and it’s rarely clear to me when to use what.As a use case, I’m trying to build a class where the logging calls of all instance methods will be prefixed with the value of a certain instance property. It doesn’t seem like the Log module supports that, so I guess I have to build a custom log method for my class which I then call. But I wonder which of the two syntaxes I should use.As an aside, I also wonder: If I doLog.trace { my_large_var.to_json } this appears to create a heap var, as the log backend abstraction is indifferent to the consumer, aka you cannot access the IO here. Logging is a use case of perhaps wanting to write to a file or in memory WAL thousands of times per second. This could be ugly wrt performance, it seems to me. Even the official performance guide recommends to work on IO instead of string operations. Am I missing something? Or should we perhaps just always instead doLog.trace &.emit(my_large_var.to_json)

item.title: Compilation on OpenBSD
item.author: karchnu
item.content:encoded:
I’ve tried to compile my project on Linux and it works great, however I have problems on OpenBSD.The default crypto library is LibreSSL and I had to use to install OpenSSL. Fine.Now, I try to compile my project and it doesn’t work, I guess because the OpenSSL library isn’t in the usual folder, headers are in /usr/local/include/eopenssl40/ and the library is in/usr/local/lib/eopenssl40.But I can’t get it to work even when using shards build myprogramm -- --linker-flags='-L /usr/local/lib/eopenssl40 -lssl -lcrypto'. I still get this error:test$ shards build authd --link-flags='-I/usr/local/include/eopenssl40/ -L/usr/local/lib/eopenssl40/ -lssl -lcrypto' Dependencies are satisfied Building: authd Error target authd failed to compile: Showing last frame. Use --error-trace for full trace.
There was a problem expanding macro 'macro_10576938180752'
Code in lib/openssl_ext/src/openssl_ext/bio/bio.cr:10:5
10 | {% if OpenSSL::BIO.has_method?(:ktls_recv?) %} ^ Called macro defined in lib/openssl_ext/src/openssl_ext/bio/bio.cr:10:5
10 | {% if OpenSSL::BIO.has_method?(:ktls_recv?) %}
Which expanded to:
1 | > 2 | Box(IO).unbox(LibCrypto.BIO_get_data(bio)) ^----------- Error: undefined fun 'BIO_get_data' for LibCrypto
The problem comes from the library (used by ).I really don’t know what to do at this point. I can write anything in the parameter, it changes absolutely nothing. But I know that the library is in /usr/local/lib/eopenssl40/ :test$ nm -g /usr/local/lib/eopenssl40/libcrypto.so.45.0 | grep BIO_get_data 00251b10 T BIO_get_data 12 posts - 5 participants

item.title: Hotwire Marten: Less JavaScript, More Interactivity

item.title: How to Set Intentions With Crystals for the First Time: A Beginner's Guide
item.author: Samriddhi Reiki Foundation (SRF)
item.content:encoded:
You have your new crystal, but now what? Learn the simple, powerful art of setting intentions and "programming" your stones with this step-by-step beginner's guide.
You Bought a Crystal... Now What? So, you have finally done it. You picked out a beautiful crystal—perhaps a soothing Rose Quartz, a vibrant Citrine, or a deeply grounding Black Tourmaline. You took it home, gave it a thorough energetic cleanse under the moonlight or on a Selenite plate, and now it is sitting perfectly on your desk.But as you look at it, a very common question arises: "Now what am I supposed to do with it?"Many beginners mistakenly believe that crystals work like magic vending machines—you simply buy one, place it in your room, and wait for peace, love, or abundance to arrive. While crystals do possess their own natural, stable energetic frequencies, their true potential is only unlocked when they are combined with one of the most powerful forces in the universe: your focused intention.Working with a crystal without setting an intention is a bit like buying a high-end smartphone but never connecting it to a network. The hardware is beautiful and capable, but it doesn't know what you want it to do.In the world of energy healing, setting an intention is often called "programming" your crystal. Here at Samriddhi Reiki Foundation, we believe this simple, conscious ritual is the essential bridge between the stone's energy and your own personal healing journey. Here is exactly how to do it for the very first time.
What Does It Actually Mean to "Set an Intention"? Before we dive into the steps, let's demystify what an intention actually is.An intention is simply a clear, focused, and purposeful thought about what you want to experience, cultivate, or release in your life. It is not a wish, and it is not a desperate plea. A wish is passive ("I hope I find love"); an intention is active, present, and empowered ("I am opening my heart to give and receive deep, healthy love").As we discussed in our previous guides, crystals possess a highly stable, perfect molecular lattice structure. In energy traditions, they are viewed as nature's ultimate energy amplifiers. Therefore, when you set an intention with a crystal, you are effectively using the stone's stable frequency to broadcast your personal goal into your energetic field and the world around you.Your mind provides the software (the specific goal), and the crystal acts as the hardware (the amplifier).
Your Step-by-Step Guide to Setting Intentions Setting an intention doesn't require a complex ceremony, hours of chanting, or years of meditation experience. It only requires your authentic focus. Here is a simple, effective method for beginners.Step 1: Cleanse Your Crystal First Never try to set a new intention into a crystal that hasn't been cleansed. Your stone may have picked up chaotic or heavy energy during its journey from the earth, to the vendor, and finally to your hands.How: Rest it on a Selenite charging plate overnight, bathe it in the smoke of sacred incense or sage, or let it sit in the light of the full moon. Start with a blank energetic slate.Step 2: Find a Quiet, Grounded Space You cannot set a clear, focused intention if your mind is racing with your grocery list or your phone is buzzing.How: Find a quiet spot where you won't be disturbed for five minutes. Sit comfortably. Take three deep, slow breaths—inhaling through your nose and exhaling with a sigh through your mouth. Allow your physical body to relax and feel grounded in your seat.Step 3: Connect With the Crystal Pick up your cleansed crystal and hold it in your non-dominant hand (in energy work, the non-dominant hand is known as the "receiving" hand, while the dominant hand is the "giving" hand).How: Close your eyes. Simply feel the weight, temperature, and texture of the stone. Imagine your own energy field gently reaching out to connect with the subtle vibration of the crystal.Step 4: Get Clear on Your Intention (Use the "I Am" Rule) Decide exactly what you want this crystal to help you with. The secret to a powerful intention is to phrase it in the present tense, as if it is already happening. Avoid words like "want," "need," or "hope," as these imply lack. Instead, use "I am" or "I choose."Examples based on crystal types: Amethyst (Calm/Sleep): "I am deeply relaxed, and my mind is peaceful and still." Rose Quartz (Love/Compassion): "I am worthy of love, and I treat myself with deep compassion." Citrine (Abundance/Joy): "I am a magnet for positive opportunities and joyful experiences." Black Tourmaline (Protection): "I am safe, grounded, and protected from energy that does not serve me."Step 5: Speak It and Visualize It (The Programming Phase) Now it is time to actively program the stone.How: While holding the crystal, state your intention clearly out loud three times. As you speak, visualize the words traveling from your mind, down your arm, and anchoring deep inside the crystal. If you are visual, you can imagine your intention as a bright beam of light filling up the stone until it glows.Step 6: Close with Gratitude Once you feel that the crystal has absorbed your intention, seal the practice.How: Bring the crystal to your heart center, take one final deep breath, and say a simple "thank you." Gratitude is one of the highest vibrational frequencies you can hold, making it the perfect way to close an energy ritual.
How to Keep Your Intention Alive Programming your crystal is just the beginning. The magic happens when you continually interact with that programmed energy in your daily life.Wear it daily: The easiest way to keep your intention active is to wear it. This is why Crystal Bracelets and Malas are so popular. Every time you glance at your wrist, it acts as a physical reminder of the intention you set. Place it intentionally: If you have a larger raw or polished stone, place it somewhere you will see it often—like your desk for focus, your nightstand for peace, or by your front door for protection.Meditate with it: Revisit your intention by holding the crystal during your morning meditation or whenever you feel stressed.
When Should You Set a New Intention? Crystals are incredibly adaptable. Once your initial intention has manifested, or if you simply feel you need support in a new area of your life, you can absolutely reprogram your stone.Simply repeat the process: give the crystal a very deep, thorough cleanse (to clear out the old programming), ground yourself, and set your brand new intention.Conclusion Working with healing crystals is ultimately a practice of profound mindfulness. By taking the time to cleanse your stone and consciously set an intention, you are stepping out of autopilot and taking an active role in creating the energy you want in your life.If you are looking for your perfect energetic companion, explore our collection of authentic, high-vibration crystals at Samriddhi Reiki Foundation. From beautifully crafted bracelets to raw stones, we have the tools you need to begin your intention-setting journey. Q: Do I have to say my intention out loud? A: You don't have to, but it is highly recommended for beginners. Speaking out loud engages your physical body and vocal cords, turning an invisible thought into a physical sound wave, which makes the programming process feel more grounded and "real."Q: Can I set more than one intention in a single crystal? A: It is best to stick to one clear, specific intention per crystal. If you give a crystal too many conflicting jobs (e.g., "Bring me money, help me sleep, and fix my relationship"), the energetic focus becomes diluted. One crystal, one job!Q: How long does an intention last in a crystal? A: An intention lasts until you purposefully clear it out, or until the crystal becomes too energetically "full" and heavy. As a general rule, you should cleanse and re-state your intention every full moon, or whenever the stone starts to feel dull or energetically "heavy."Q: What if I don't feel anything when setting my intention? A: That is completely normal! Energy work is subtle. You don't need to feel a massive electric shock or have a cinematic vision for the process to work. Trust your intention, trust the stone, and allow the subtle shifts to unfold over time.
